timeline.label: exposure partially recovers, scene still washed
first usable frame: ego stopped at parking-lot barrier/gate
after ~130 s, ego still at identical barrier pose (stuck scene)
ego has finally moved onto adjacent street

hypotheses.summary: Ego vehicle remains at the same parking-lot barrier/gate pose for ~130 s (frames 20 s–150 s are visually near-identical) before proceeding, suggesting a prolonged idle/stuck state at the gate.

evidence.snippet: Ego stopped facing boom barrier with parked cars on left and gate ticket booth ahead.
Identical viewpoint 30 s later — same cars, same barrier, same pedestrian position — no measurable ego motion.
Still identical parking-lot/barrier scene ~80 s after first usable frame.
Scene finally changes — ego has departed onto adjacent residential street.

hypotheses.patch_hint: Check planner/controller behavior at closed gates; add timeout + operator alert if ego is stationary >N s with no lead obstacle within stop distance.

hypotheses.summary: Initial ~5 s of camera stream is severely overexposed (near-pure-white), indicating auto-exposure convergence failure or recording started before ISP stabilized.

evidence.snippet: Almost entirely saturated white image; only faint foliage edges visible.
Still heavily blown out; partial recovery, parked cars barely resolvable at edges.
Exposure has normalized — scene now correctly exposed.

hypotheses.patch_hint: Reject / mask perception outputs until AE has converged (e.g., gate on image mean-luma + histogram saturation ratio) and/or pre-warm the camera before autonomy-engage.

patch_proposal: Primary concern: ego remained stationary at the parking-lot barrier for ~130 s — validate whether the planner correctly detected a closed gate and whether human takeover was required; add a stuck-state detector with operator alert. Secondary: guard perception consumers against the ~5 s of saturated-white camera frames at session start (likely AE not yet converged).
